title: put() new-object path makes two nvm_list round-trips
description: When put() creates a new object (find_cached_or_scan returns None), it calls find_free_id which issues a second nvm_list round-trip to the HSM. The first nvm_list already ran inside find_by_label (called from find_cached_or_scan). On an embedded HSM over a socket, each round-trip has non-trivial latency. Fix: have find_cached_or_scan return the full Vec<NvmId> list on a cache miss so find_free_id can consume it directly instead of calling nvm_list again.
